claim: Karpathy's Neural Networks: Zero to Hero is a structured course with 8 lectures spanning micrograd backprop through GPT and tokenizer construction.
quote: The course starts with the basics of backpropagation, then proceeds to multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs), convolutional neural networks (CNNs), and finally builds up to modern deep neural networks like generative pre-trained transformers (GPTs).

claim: The Zero to Hero playlist is hosted on Andrej Karpathy's official YouTube channel.
quote: Andrej Karpathy Neural Networks: Zero to Hero

claim: Karpathy posted a 3h31m 'Deep Dive into LLMs like ChatGPT' general-audience lecture covering the full LLM training stack.
quote: New 3h31m video on YouTube: "Deep Dive into LLMs like ChatGPT" This is a general audience deep dive into the Large Language Model (LLM) AI technology that powers ChatGPT and related products. It is covers the full training stack of how the models are developed

claim: Karpathy's 'Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M)' is a 4-hour from-scratch lecture released June 2024 that builds, optimizes and trains a GPT-2 model end-to-end.
quote: New 4 hour (lol) video lecture on YouTube: "Let's reproduce GPT-2 (124M)"... we start with empty file and end up with a GPT-2 (124M) model

claim: Stanford CS25 Transformers United is on its sixth iteration in 2026 and remains an open-audit seminar with rotating frontier speakers.
quote: Now on our sixth iteration of the course... CS25 has become one of Stanford's hottest seminar courses, featuring top researchers at the forefront of Transformers research such as Geoffrey Hinton, Ashish Vaswani, and Andrej Karpathy.

claim: CS25's full lecture playlist (V1 through V6) is publicly available on Stanford Online's YouTube and posts new lectures within days of recording.
quote: Stanford CS25 - Transformers United

claim: CS25 V6 'From Representation Learning to World Modeling' was published in April 2026, demonstrating active weekly drops during the spring quarter.
quote: Stanford CS25: Transformers United V6 I From Representation Learning to World Modeling

claim: MIT 6.S191 publishes a fully refreshed lecture set every January IAP, with the 2026 edition graded P/D/F as a 3-unit MIT course.
quote: In 2026, 6.S191 is being offered as a for-credit 3-unit MIT course graded P/D/F based on completion of project proposal assignment.

claim: The MIT 6.S191 YouTube playlist hosts the canonical Alexander Amini and Ava Amini lectures including the 2026 'Foundations of Deep Learning' refresh.
quote: MIT 6.S191: Introduction to Deep Learning

claim: Stanford CS224N 'NLP with Deep Learning' under Christopher Manning remains the canonical NLP lecture series with full Spring 2024 videos on the Stanford Online YouTube playlist.
quote: Stanford CS 224N | Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning

claim: Stanford CS231N 'Deep Learning for Computer Vision' continues to publish new yearly recordings (Spring 2025 lectures live) and is the standard CV reference course.
quote: Stanford University CS231n: Deep Learning for Computer Vision

claim: DeepLearning.AI's YouTube channel, founded by Andrew Ng in 2017, hosts Coursera lecture excerpts plus a steady stream of free 1–2 hour 'short courses' co-built with OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, Google and AWS.
quote: The short courses are free, each 1-2 hours long covering specific GenAI topics, and they're built in partnership with major AI companies like OpenAI, LangChain, Anthropic, Google and AWS.

claim: Google DeepMind's official YouTube channel hosts 'Google DeepMind: The Podcast' with Hannah Fry plus product/research keynote drops, returning with new episodes in 2026.
quote: Google DeepMind

claim: The Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing publishes its semester programs, boot camps and workshops (including the 2026 Federated and Collaborative Learning program) as full-length talks on YouTube.
quote: Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing

claim: Microsoft Research's YouTube channel hosts the Microsoft Research Forum series of recorded talks alongside lab seminars across AI, theory and AI-for-science.
quote: Microsoft Research

claim: Sebastian Raschka's YouTube channel pairs his 'Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)' code-along playlist with 2026 commentary videos on RLVR, GRPO and reasoning-LLM trends.
quote: Sebastian Raschka - YouTube

claim: Jeremy Howard's fast.ai 'Practical Deep Learning for Coders' is a free 8-lesson, ~11-hour video course distributed on YouTube and the fast.ai site.
quote: Practical Deep Learning - Fast.ai

claim: Hugging Face's official YouTube channel publishes the Hugging Face Course playlist plus community talks tied to its open-model and dataset releases.
quote: Hugging Face

claim: Reddit's r/MachineLearning consistently surfaces Stanford CS229, CS231N and Karpathy's lectures as the highest-rigor free video courses, with 95+ mentions for CS229 alone.
quote: Stanford's CS229 earned 95+ mentions in r/MachineLearning as the most rigorous free ML course... diving deep into mathematical foundations, optimization theory, and probabilistic models.

claim: The community-maintained dair-ai 'ML-YouTube-Courses' index aggregates the canonical free ML lecture series on YouTube including Stanford, MIT, DeepMind and CMU offerings.
quote: Discover the latest machine learning / AI courses on YouTube.
